pelodiscussinensisPH

Axela Keeza T. Ugalde

2026-06-25

Activity 10 - Geographic Information System: Creating Your First Map

A. Retrieving spatial data

library(sf)
## Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE
library(terra)
## terra 1.9.34
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:terra':
## 
##     intersect, union
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(ggplot2)
library(ggnewscale)
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.2.2     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ tidyr::extract() masks terra::extract()
## ✖ dplyr::filter()  masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()     masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
# Read CSV
my_data <- read.csv("occurrence - Pelodiscus sinensis.csv")

# Clean and Subset
clean_data <- my_data |>
  filter(!is.na(decimalLatitude) & !is.na(decimalLongitude))

target_provinces <- st_read("PHL_adm1.shp") |>
  st_transform(4326) |>
  st_make_valid()
## Reading layer `PHL_adm1' from data source 
##   `/Users/akee/Downloads/DLSU/SY 25-26/Term 3/Academics/THESIS/PHL_adm1.shp' 
##   using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
## Simple feature collection with 81 features and 9 fields
## Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON
## Dimension:     XY
## Bounding box:  xmin: 116.9283 ymin: 4.58694 xmax: 126.6053 ymax: 21.07014
## Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84
# Elevation
phil_dem <- rast("ph_ strm_geo.tif")
dem_resampled <- aggregate(phil_dem, fact = 4, fun = mean)
## |---------|---------|---------|---------|=========================================                                          
if (st_crs(target_provinces) != st_crs(dem_resampled)) {
  target_provinces <- st_transform(target_provinces, st_crs(dem_resampled))
}
dem_clipped <- crop(dem_resampled, target_provinces) |> mask(target_provinces)

dem_df <- as.data.frame(dem_clipped, xy = TRUE)
colnames(dem_df) <- c("x", "y", "elevation")

plot(dem_clipped)

B. Map

ggplot() +
  geom_tile(data = dem_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = elevation)) +
 scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("#1a4d2e", "#4f772d", "#90a955", "#ecf39e", "#f3c68f", "#ee6c4d", "#c32f27"),
    values = c(0, 0.05, 0.15, 0.30, 0.50, 0.75, 1.0), 
    name = "Elevation (m)"
  ) +

  geom_sf(data = target_provinces, fill = NA, 
          color = "gray30", 
          linewidth = 0.1) +
  
  geom_point(data = clean_data, aes(x = decimalLongitude, y = decimalLatitude), 
             shape = 21, 
             fill = "hotpink", 
             color = "black", 
             size = 2.0, 
             stroke = 0.5) +

  coord_sf() + 
  labs(title = expression(paste("National Distribution of ", italic("Pelodiscus sinensis"))),
       subtitle = "GBIF Data Philippines",
       x = "Longitude (°E)", y = "Latitude (°N)" +
  
  theme_linedraw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "aliceblue"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", 
                                    linetype = "dashed", 
                                    linewidth = 0.3)
  ))

ggsave("philippines_distribution_map.pdf", 
       width = 8, 
       height = 11, 
       units = "in", 
       dpi = 300)
